parsed_review.summary: CP00-645 is a descriptor-only Risk C closeout pack (150 units, RP21.P00.M00.S01-RP21.P01.M08.S05) opening the RP21 Admin Console scope/domain foundation. Read-only inspection via Read/Grep/Glob confirms the pack is internally consistent and its (pending) production_ready posture is supported by implementation, tests, and local/contract validation. Verified: (1) Pack binding (packages/admin/src/registry.js ADMIN_CONSOLE_CP645_PACK_BINDING) matches the review baseline, the contract projection in contracts/admin-console-contract.json (program/current_pack/cp645_requirements/cp645_no_write_attestation/cp645_descriptor/cp645_*_packet/cp645_closeout_handoff), the manifest plan_binding_snapshot, closeout-pack-plan.json live_state, and next-pack-queue.json (live_latest CP00-645, next CP00-646, live_next_unit RP21.P01.M08.S06); the CP00-644->CP00-645->CP00-646 chain and next_subphase RP21.P01.M08.S06 are consistent. (2) Deliverable distribution (implementation 104, contract 3, security_audit 6, ui 19, fixture 3, test 9, hermes_evidence 3, claude_review 3 = 150) and the 11 micro-title counts match the prompt baseline, registry requirements, and contract; validateAdminConsoleCp645Coverage enforces deliverable/phase/micro-phase/micro-title counts and per-section row titles against the 150-unit plan snapshot (CP00-645 is the live pack captured in live_state + manifest.plan_binding_snapshot; the test/validator fall back to that snapshot, which carries the full 150 units). (3) freezeCp645Result and ADMIN_CONSOLE_CP645_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION set all writes_product_state / executes_admin_console|taxonomy|template|workflow|policy|usage_limit|billing_plan_runtime / evaluates_runtime_permission / writes_permission_decision / emits|persists_audit_event / creates|updates|deletes_database_rows / reads|writes_object_storage / opens_ui_runtime / exposes_hidden_policy_internals / exposes_unauthorized_admin_rows / leak_detected / permission_bypass_detected flags to false; validators.js asserts every false flag plus descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, raw_payload_included=false per row, the five no_leak_guards, no Claude final-approval promotion, and contract runtime_opened=false; model.test.js asserts validity, boundary flags, and contract-projection deep-equality. The admin package source contains no real I/O (no DB, network, child_process, or filesystem writes); the only matches for executes_* are boolean descriptor flags set false. (4) command-evidence.json records 21 commands all exiting 0 (node --check x7, closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, rp21:admin-console:validate, rp21:validate, node --test, weighted/spec/fullplan/validate/goal:closeout/rp00:control-plane:validate, npm test, npm run build, git diff --check); readiness-report.json auth preflight passed with a valid JSON result (no auth failure, 0-byte stdout, malformed/fenced JSON, or tool-call-shaped output). (5) No premature production_ready or enterprise-trust claim: manifest production_ready=false/runtime_ready=false/status in_progress, adjudication 'Production ready after adjudication: no', construction-inspection verdict PENDING_REVIEW with admin_console_scope_domain_foundation_descriptor_verified=false, claude-review-result status pending_review with empty valid_review_receipts/invalid_review_attempts, and no pre-baked raw-output.json/review-receipt.json. (6) Claude remains read-only and non-approving (read_only=true, allowed_tools read-only, forbidden_review_evidence covers not_logged_in/malformed_json/quota_failure/permission_denied/tool_call_only_output, claude_final_approval_claimed/promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false), Hermes patterns are descriptor/receipt-only (runtime_receipt_emitted=false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, hermes_runtime_opened=false, not embedded in product runtime), and human final approval remains required for any enterprise-trust/runtime-opening claim. One non-blocking P3: scripts/validate-rp21-admin-console-contract.mjs bundles a gated --write contract-generation path (unlike the strictly read-only RP20 data-room validator), but it is gated behind --write, not invoked by npm run rp21:admin-console:validate, and writes only the deterministic contract-projection artifact (no product/runtime state, audit/permission decisions, object storage, or real data), staying within the deterministic control-plane projection pattern. This review is advisory read-only evidence, not final approval. No P0/P1/P2 findings.

findings.title: RP21 admin-console validator bundles a gated --write contract-generation path (new vs. read-only RP20 precedent)
findings.details: The validator imports writeFile from node:fs/promises (line 2) and, when invoked with the --write argument (shouldWrite, line 23), overwrites contracts/admin-console-contract.json with the generated projection (lines 35-37). This differs from the prior RP20 data-room validator (scripts/validate-rp20-data-room-vdr-core-contract.mjs), which imports only readFile and is strictly read-only. Verified the write path is gated: package.json maps rp21:admin-console:validate to 'node scripts/validate-rp21-admin-console-contract.mjs' with no --write, so the CI/validation path is read-only, and the only artifact ever written is the deterministic contract projection (contracts/admin-console-contract.json) which the test then deep-equals against the in-code projection. No product/runtime state, audit/permission decision, object-storage, or real-data write occurs, so the no-write attestation and the deterministic control-plane contract-projection pattern are not violated.
findings.recommendation: Keep the default validator read-only (already the case via the npm script). Optionally split the --write contract generation into a clearly named separate generate script (e.g. generate-rp21-admin-console-contract.mjs) so the validator surface stays read-only by construction, matching the RP20 precedent and reducing the chance of an accidental artifact overwrite during validation.
findings.post_review_disposition: Acknowledged, non-blocking. Accepted as deterministic control-plane contract-projection tooling (dev/CI artifact generation, not product runtime); does not affect the descriptor-only / no-write / no-real-data boundaries or the production_ready posture. Recorded for the human final approver; no remediation required for CP00-645 closeout.
